title: iris caucasica subsp. caucasica
intro: Iris caucasica subsp. caucasica, also known as the Caucasian iris, is a flowering plant species native to the Caucasus region, characterized by its blue-violet flowers with yellow and white markings. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its iridin content. The health benefits of Iris caucasica subsp. caucasica include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing. Iris caucasica subsp. caucasica can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
